description_long: This object is a tall, stone guard stone featuring a prominent cross at the top, with intricate Celtic knotwork and patterns carved into its surface. The shaft is rectangular and tapers slightly as it rises from a broad, weathered base, which appears to be set on a roughly square plinth. The stone shows signs of age, with patches of moss and discoloration, especially near the base, suggesting long-term outdoor exposure. The cross itself is decorated with interlacing motifs, typical of historical or commemorative markers. The overall form is monolithic and robust, designed to serve as a protective or boundary marker in an urban or historical setting. The craftsmanship and ornamentation indicate it may also have cultural or memorial significance.
description: A tall, weathered stone guard stone with a Celtic cross and intricate carvings, set on a broad, aged base. The surface shows moss and discoloration, highlighting its historical and protective role.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile of the shaft and the cross, with visible moss and weathering on the base.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full detail of the Celtic cross and intricate carvings along the shaft, with the broadest face of the base visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the narrow profile and additional weathering on the base.
description_view_3: Rear view of the cross and shaft, with less visible ornamentation and more pronounced discoloration on the base.
attribute_location: Typically found at the entrance to historical sites, along urban sidewalks, or near the corners of old stone walls, often marking boundaries or protecting corners from vehicle impact.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright on a flat surface, sometimes partially embedded in the ground for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible discoloration and moss growth at the base, with some surface smoothing and minor chipping on edges due to weathering and possible contact with vehicles or foot traffic. The carvings show signs of erosion, especially on the most exposed faces.
attribute_historical_markings: Intricate Celtic knotwork and cross motifs carved into the shaft and cross, with no visible inscriptions or text. The style is consistent with historical or commemorative markers.
